description: §61.7 — an `<endpoint>` that omits `method=` entirely fires E-ENDPOINT-METHOD-INVALID (a missing method literal is reported by the same code, not a distinct one). The handler needs a method to mount for.
rationale: §61.7: `method=` SHALL be a recognized HTTP method literal; a missing method= has no recognized literal, so it fires E-ENDPOINT-METHOD-INVALID (parse-time, W2 — the same code as an unrecognized value).
